cover_letter:
Dear Hiring Team,

I'm applying for the Low-Code Engineer role at Unison Group. Your focus on streamlining business processes through automated workflows matches exactly what I've been building, both professionally and on my own time.

At EXAIL Robotics, I redesigned our software installation process. It used to take about 2 days. Now it takes 2 hours and anyone on the team can run it. That's an 85% reduction in deployment time. I built this automation using scripting and process optimization, making complex technical steps accessible to non-technical users. I also develop core features in C++ and JavaScript, including an eye-tracking system, so I understand both the business logic and the technical implementation.

Outside of work, I built ValoFenua, a real estate platform that automates daily scraping of 1,300+ listings and processes them through AI-driven workflows. I handle the full stack, from React frontend to PostgreSQL databases, and I'm comfortable with REST APIs and third-party integrations. While I haven't used OutSystems or Mendix specifically, I've built complex automation pipelines with n8n and understand how to translate business requirements into technical specifications without heavy coding.

I'm finishing my M.S. in Information Systems Engineering in 2026. I like the idea of helping business teams move faster without getting blocked by traditional development bottlenecks.

Aodren Gloux

stack: C/C++, Python, C#, Robotics, Hardware Integration, OOP

Direct alignment with current C++ robotics software development at EXAIL
Hardware integration and field testing experience fits the role requirements

commentaire: Strong match for an entry-level robotics software role requiring C++ and hardware integration experience. The position directly leverages current work on eye-tracking systems and deployment automation.

cover_letter:
Dear Hiring Team,

I'm applying for the Software Engineer, Infrastructure role at Exa. You're building a search engine from scratch to power AI applications. That's the kind of deep technical infrastructure I want to work on.

I currently develop software at EXAIL Robotics in C++ and JavaScript, including an eye-tracking system. I also redesigned our installation process. It used to take about 2 days. Now it takes 2 hours. Anyone on the team can run it. That's an 85% reduction in deployment time.

On my own time, I built ValoFenua, a real estate platform for French Polynesia. It scrapes 1,300+ listings daily and feeds them into an AI agent for price estimation. I also built an automated job search pipeline with n8n, AI agents, and an MCP server on a VPS. I know Docker and have managed VPS environments. I'm comfortable with the automation and infrastructure side.

I'm finishing my M.S. in Information Systems Engineering in 2026. Exa is tackling massive scale problems with GPU clusters and vector search. I want to help build that infrastructure.

Aodren Gloux

points_positifs: Entry level position perfectly matching Aodren's experience level
Target location Singapore
Combines React (ValoFenua) and C++ (EXAIL) skills
High-growth AI company with $250M revenue run rate
Strong benefits including ESOP and best-in-class equipment

cover_letter:
Dear Hiring Team,

I'm applying for the Frontend Engineer (Desktop) role at Plaud. Your work on AI-powered note-taking solutions sits at the intersection of software engineering and practical AI integration. That is exactly where I want to build.

At EXAIL Robotics, I develop core features in C++ and JavaScript, including an eye-tracking system. I also redesigned our installation process. It used to take about 2 days. Now it takes 2 hours. That is an 85% reduction in deployment time, and anyone on the team can run it now. On my own time, I built ValoFenua, a React application with Supabase and PostgreSQL that processes 1,300+ daily data points through AI agents.

You are looking for someone comfortable with both Electron and C++. I have shipped production C++ code and built complex React applications. I can handle the performance constraints of desktop applications while keeping the UI responsive and intuitive.

I am finishing my M.S. in Information Systems Engineering in 2026. Plaud's approach to capturing and utilizing intelligence through hardware-software combinations is compelling. I would welcome the chance to contribute to your desktop architecture.

Aodren Gloux
